page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, the instance features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled on an x86_64 architecture. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and a 55 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the e2-highmem-4 delivers modest compute performance. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier, with scores of 1106.18 and 2891.86 respectively. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are positioned in the bottom 10% tier. Memory performance is relatively stronger, with a PassMark memory score of 1969.62 placing it in the average middle 50% tier, though large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidths are limited to 24.7 GB/sec and 21.34 GB/sec.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core alongside lower-tier compute performance, the e2-highmem-4 is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require intensive CPU processing. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring a larger memory footprint without the expense of high-performance compute cores. Suitable workloads include caching layers, small database operations, and lightweight text or prompt processing, as demonstrated by its average-tier text generation speed of 102.59 tokens per second for small 135M models.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor. Operating at 2.2 GHz with 2 physical cores and hyper-threading, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks position its compute capabilities in the lower tiers, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling in the bottom 10% to 25%. However, its memory subsystem achieves average-tier performance, highlighted by a PassMark memory score of 1969.62. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for caching systems, small database operations, and basic text processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering a cost-effective profile for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highmem-4 is an x86_64 instance with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for cost-conscious, memory-intensive workloads without heavy compute demands.
bullet_points: Configured with 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory
Powered by an Intel Xeon 2.2 GHz processor with 2 physical cores
Delivers average-tier memory performance with a 1969.62 PassMark score
Lacks local storage and GPU hardware acceleration
Best suited for caching, small databases, and lightweight text processing
